title: NEWS: Alaska Legislature Urges Congress to Restore State’s Full Share of Federal Resource Revenues
summary: JUNEAU, AK – Today, the Alaska House passed Senate Joint Resolution 19 , sponsored by Senator Jesse Bjorkman (R-Nikiski), encouraging Congress to honor Alaska’s birthright of the 90/10 split of revenue from mineral production on federal lands, as guaranteed by the Alaska Statehood Compact. The post...

title: NEWS: Alaska Legislature Passes Legislation to Streamline Medical Prior Authorizations
summary: JUNEAU, AK – Today, the Alaska House unanimously passed Senate Bill 133 . Sponsored by Senator Jesse Bjorkman (R-Nikiski) through the Labor and Commerce Committee that he chairs. The bill will make it faster and easier for Alaskans to get medical care and prescriptions approved by their health insur...

title: NEWS: Alaska Legislature Passes Resolution Urging Congress to Act on the Ensuring Lasting Smiles Act
summary: JUNEAU, AK – Today, the Alaska State Legislature passed Senate Joint Resolution 16 , sponsored by Senator Elvi Gray-Jackson , D-Anchorage, a resolution urging Congress to advance the federal Ensuring Lasting Smiles Act, a federal policy proposal aimed at improving access to medically necessary treat...

title: Budget Conference Committee process in Depth and lots more!

title: NEWS: Legislature Passes Bill Expanding Access to Affordable Child Care
summary: JUNEAU – Today, the Alaska Legislature passed Senate Bill 95 , sponsored by the Senate Health & Social Services Committee, to improve access to affordable childcare for working families and strengthen Alaska’s workforce. title: AST Assault 4: Fairbanks [AK25047338]
summary: On 05/18/2025 at approximately 0731 hours, Troopers responded to the Chena Pump boat launch to multiple 911 calls reporting a female lying in the roadway possibly needing medical assistance. Investigation revealed two females under the legal drinking age of 21 were intoxicated, and one was detained. Lilyanne Jelinek became agitated, demanding troopers release the other female and swinging at one trooper, and was charged with Assault in the Fourth Degree.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

summary: On 05/18/2025, at approximately 0108 hours, Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle bearing plate KPV656. The driver, Michael Carroll, age 22 of Fairbanks, was arrested for driving under the influence and misconduct involving weapons in the fourth degree.
